notes_for_consumers: Feeds environment prompts and footage queries. Northeast is colder and WETTER than Great Lakes (not lake-effect dry) with a denser, older, colonial built environment and a professional/trades-skewed audience. Year-End Q4 sits in the bare-branch / first-snow transition; fall foliage is the signature seasonal visual. Pick season-correct content; the high-confidence climate-and-landscape layer is operative, the low-confidence sensory mood is creative reference.

winter.retail_moment: Big Finish (Dec), Black Friday (Nov), Start Something New (Jan)
winter.weather: cold and wet; coastal Maine and northern New England see real winter; mid-Atlantic and coastal MA/CT/RI see mixed precipitation, slush, freezing rain; upstate NY sees lake-effect off Erie/Ontario; marine influence moderates but humidity makes cold cut harder
winter.light: short days, low-angle sun, often overcast; coastal salt-marine quality; inland flatter
winter.landscape: older built environment (colonial-era town centers, brick-and-clapboard housing, stone walls, narrow rural roads, granite/slate); bare deciduous (maples, oaks, beech); dark conifer in upper NE; salted/brine-treated roads
winter.activity_rhythm: trades and construction continue (interior work, snowplow, generator season); hunting upstate NY/PA/ME; ice fishing smaller lakes; coastal oyster/lobster work; holiday hospitality
winter.cgi_footage_implication: Colder and wetter than Great Lakes — more salt streak, more ice-glaze, brine on asphalt; architecture reads colonial-and-dense, not rural-Midwest open.

spring.retail_moment: Declaration of Deals (Mar-Apr), 4x4 Day, Truck Month
spring.weather: slow thaw; March still cold and wet; April first real warming + rapid tree-leaf emergence; May warms quickly; coastal lags inland by weeks; mud season real rural NE; coastal nor'easters into April
spring.light: lengthening fast, late-afternoon golden return; spring color (bright greens, redbud, dogwood, magnolia) against still-bare overstory
spring.landscape: stone walls re-emerging from snow; streams running high; maple sugaring in northern NE into March; lawns muddy then greening; boats coming out of storage
spring.activity_rhythm: trades season ramps (residential construction, landscaping, marine yards); fishing opens; garden centers surge

summer.retail_moment: Make This The Summer Event, Summer of Jeep
summer.weather: warm and humid; coastal marine moderation (Cape, Long Island, Newport, RI shoreline); inland and upstate hot/sticky; afternoon thunderstorms; hurricane risk coast Aug-Oct
summer.light: long days, full canopy filtering; late golden hour into evening; coastal luminous; inland sometimes hazy
summer.landscape: full deciduous canopy mature; coastal salt marsh, dune, beach grass, harbor; Finger Lakes, Champlain, Berkshires, Adirondacks, White Mountains busy
summer.activity_rhythm: construction peak; boating/sailing season; beach recreation; summer-stock theater, fairs, festivals; weekend rural escapes from urban metros

fall.weather: cooling fast; leaf-peeping Oct in northern NE (Vermont, NH, Maine), Oct-early Nov in lower NE/mid-Atlantic; first frost mid-Oct in north; November turns gray and cold quickly; nor'easter risk into October
fall.light: clear, sharp, warming-color palette; bright peak leaf color (orange, red, yellow, gold) for a 2-3 week window
fall.landscape: foliage peak is the dominant visual; pumpkin patches, apple orchards, cider mills, hayrides at peak; late fall bare branches, gray late-Nov
fall.activity_rhythm: deer season; NFL Sundays, high school Friday lights, college Saturdays; apple harvest; trades winterizing properties; boats out of water; firewood cut and stacked
